summary: DCSS ASCII build for WebAssembly via Emscripten, focusing on console-only rendering without SDL/tiles

lua.CRITICAL_RESTRICTION: LuaJIT is FORBIDDEN - WebAssembly cannot execute dynamically generated machine code (JIT compilation) due to browser security sandboxes. You MUST use standard interpreted C-Lua from contrib/lua, NOT contrib/luajit.
lua.build_action: use contrib lua (BUILD_LUA=yes). Ensure Makefile does NOT use luajit.
lua.emscripten_notes: Use contrib/lua (C implementation). Build as part of contrib. LuaJIT will not work in Wasm.

sqlite.location: USE EMSCRIPTEN BUILT-IN - DO NOT compile contrib/sqlite
sqlite.build_action: Add -s USE_SQLITE3=1 to linker flags. Bypass contrib/sqlite entirely.
sqlite.emscripten_notes: Emscripten has a highly optimized pre-compiled SQLite3 port built-in. Use -s USE_SQLITE3=1 instead of BUILD_SQLITE=yes. This avoids fighting the DCSS Makefiles to compile contrib/sqlite to Wasm.

ncurses_curses.emscripten_notes: MUST REPLACE with pdcurses or custom terminal output layer. Emscripten has no native curses support.
ncurses_curses.alternative: Implement custom 'curses-compatible' output using JavaScript console/canvas

zlib.emscripten_notes: Use Emscripten's built-in zlib (s拼搏orts.js includes zlib)

pcre.emscripten_notes: Use POSIX regex instead (-DREGEX_POSIX) to avoid PCRE porting effort

platform_headers: platform.h - already handles multiple OS detection
threads.h - pthread abstraction (needs replacement for Wasm)

threading_model.issue: Emscripten's pthreads require SharedArrayBuffer which has security restrictions
threading_model.recommendation: Either: 1) Use Emscripten's -s USE_PTHREADS=1 with proper headers, or 2) Replace with single-threaded model using async/await in JS glue code

usage_patterns: Save files: get_save_filename(), save_dir in options.h
file_io_operations.emscripten_solution: Use Emscripten's virtual filesystem (MEMFS or IDBFS)
critical_functions: fopen_u() - user file open with path resolution
fopen_replace() - open for writing with path resolution
file_io_operations.persistent_storage: Use Emscripten's IDBFS for IndexedDB-backed persistent saves

signal_handling.usage: Signal handlers for crashes in dbg-asrt.cc
signal_handling.issue: Unix signals don't exist in browser environment
signal_handling.recommendation: Wrap in #ifndef __EMSCRIPTEN__ blocks or provide JS exception handling

terminal_io.issue: curses library not available in Wasm browser
terminal_io.recommended_approach: RECOMMENDED: Use PDCurses (Public Domain Curses) which has existing Emscripten ports. ALTERNATIVE: Pipe raw character output to xterm.js on the JavaScript side. AVOID: Writing custom curses-shim.h from scratch (will take weeks).
terminal_io.pdcurses_notes: PDCurses has mature Emscripten support - much faster to integrate than custom shim.

threads.changes: Replace pthread with Wasm-compatible threading or stub out

libunix.changes: Major: Replace curses implementation with browser terminal shim

main.changes: Handle init_signals() stub, add Emscripten main loop

cmake_approach.note: DCSS uses Makefile, not CMake. For Emscripten, create custom Makefile.emscripten
cmake_approach.emcc_usage: Replace $(CXX) and $(CC) with emcc/em++ in custom makefile

build_steps: 1. Create Makefile.emscripten based on existing Makefile
3. Integrate PDCurses (preferred) or create minimal curses wrapper - NOT full custom shim
4. Replace threading in threads.h with stub or emscripten_pthread_shim (or use single-threaded)
5. Add Emscripten filesystem wrappers in files.cc (MEMFS for temp, IDBFS for saves)
6. Build Lua from contrib/lua ONLY (NO luajit). Build zlib via contrib.
7. Use -s USE_SQLITE3=1 instead of compiling contrib/sqlite
8. Build with: make -f Makefile.emscripten (or invoke make with EMSCRIPTEN=1)
9. Generate HTML shell and link JavaScript glue code

testing_notes.browser_requirements: SharedArrayBuffer support requires cross-origin isolation headers
testing_notes.fallback: If SharedArrayBuffer unavailable, build single-threaded (no -s USE_PTHREADS=1)

risks_and_notes: curses/terminal I/O is the biggest challenge - no direct Wasm equivalent
Browser sandbox prevents direct file system access - must use Emscripten FS
Lua scripting in browser has security implications - consider sandboxing
Save game persistence requires IndexedDB via Emscripten IDBFS
Input handling needs to bridge browser keyboard events to game
No sound support in ASCII build (fine for this scope)

tiles_port.summary: Tiles (graphical) WASM build, parallel to the ASCII target. End-to-end playable in a browser: chargen -> dungeon descent -> save/load survives tab reload via IDBFS. Native tiles build is untouched - every change is gated on __EMSCRIPTEN__ or lives in a parallel build file.
tiles_port.html_shell: index_tiles.html (canvas-based; SDL2 emcc port reads Module.canvas and routes DOM keyboard/mouse events automatically)

additional_dependencies.sdl2: Emscripten port via --use-port=sdl2 (NOT contrib/sdl2 cross-compiled to WASM)
additional_dependencies.libpng: Emscripten port via --use-port=libpng (also pulls Emscripten's zlib transitively, so BUILD_ZLIB= is dropped to avoid wasm-ld symbol collisions)
additional_dependencies.idbfs: -lidbfs.js explicit in newer Emscripten (no longer included by default)
additional_dependencies.host_libpng: libpng-dev installed in Dockerfile so the rltiles host build can link tilegen.elf via pkg-config

gl_strategy.rationale: DCSS's glwrapper-ogl.cc is GL 1.x fixed-function (glEnableClientState, glVertexPointer, glColor4f, matrix stack, glAlphaFunc) - none of which exist in WebGL. LEGACY_GL_EMULATION is a documented slow/buggy path; a small shader rewrite is faster and easier to debug.
gl_strategy.implementation: glwrapper-ogl.cc gains a #ifdef __EMSCRIPTEN__ path with two GLSL ES 3.00 programs (textured-modulated, untextured-colored), manual mat4 stack replacing PROJECTION/MODELVIEW, alpha test as a fragment-shader discard, dynamic VBO/IBO uploads in OGLShapeBuffer::draw. dcss_es2 namespace at the top of the file holds the helpers. Native fixed-function path stays under #else.
gl_strategy.webgl_version: MIN_WEBGL_VERSION=2 MAX_WEBGL_VERSION=2 (WebGL2 for NPOT textures + GLSL ES 3.00 / texture() builtin)

files_modified.crawl-ref/source/Dockerfile: added libpng-dev so the rltiles host build (system g++) can link tilegen.elf via pkg-config
files_modified.crawl-ref/source/Makefile: added a single new gate NO_SDL_LIBS that suppresses 'LIBS += -lSDL2_image $(SDL2_LDFLAGS) $(FREETYPE_LDFLAGS)' when libraries come from emcc ports rather than system or contrib
files_modified.crawl-ref/source/glwrapper-ogl.cc: GLES2 shader path under __EMSCRIPTEN__; extended GL_CLAMP guard to skip the legacy enum (defined by GLES3 headers but rejected at runtime by WebGL); guarded the SDL_GL_LoadLibrary mipmap probe out under Emscripten
files_modified.crawl-ref/source/windowmanager-sdl.cc: Emscripten guards on SDL_GetGlobalMouseState (single display assumed), the RESIZABLE/HIGHDPI/FULLSCREEN window flags, set_window_icon (no-op), set_mouse_cursor (no-op), clipboard get/has (stubs)
files_modified.crawl-ref/source/fs_init.js: fixed IDBFS mount paths to /crawl/saves and /crawl/morgue (DCSS runs with -dir /crawl, so its actual save paths are nested - the previous /saves and /morgue mounts never intercepted writes)

host_tool_handling.rltiles: Built with the host g++ via the host-rltiles target before invoking the emcc parent build. Generates dat/tiles/*.png (sprite sheets) and tiledef-*.{h,cc} (tile index headers/sources) at build time. The parent Makefile re-runs build-rltiles too, but with libpng-dev installed it links cleanly via pkg-config without needing the contrib path.
host_tool_handling.critical: NO_PKGCONFIG must NOT be set on the parent Makefile invocation. With NO_PKGCONFIG=YesPlease, rltiles falls back to contrib/install/$(ARCH)/lib/libpng.a where $(ARCH) is wasm32-unknown-emscripten under emcc - a path we don't populate, so host link of tilegen.elf fails.

tiles_port.compile_AND_link_flags: EMCC_PORT_FLAGS (--use-port=sdl2/sdl2_image/freetype/libpng) MUST be present at BOTH compile and link stages. Compile-time activates the SDL2 port headers (without it, fakesdl/SDL_keycode.h #errors out). The build harness passes them via DEFINES (CFOTHERS) and EMCC_LDFLAGS.
deferred_features: Audio: USE_SOUND= stays empty (browser autoplay policies; not core to playability)
Mouse picking / click-to-attack: SDL events flow but click-to-move is disabled
Window resize / fullscreen toggle: stripped via flag mask
Mipmaps under WebGL: SDL_GL_LoadLibrary probe guarded out; not load-bearing visually
tiles_port.font_path_handling: PROPORTIONAL_FONT and MONOSPACED_FONT are passed as Make variables (not -D defines) so the parent Makefile (lines 1011-1031) emits its own -DPROPORTIONAL_FONT="..." pointing at /fonts/DejaVuSans.ttf and /fonts/DejaVuSansMono.ttf. The fonts themselves are bundled from contrib/fonts/ via --preload-file contrib/fonts@/fonts. Without the Make-variable approach, the parent runs util/find_font and resolves to /usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/* which doesn't exist in the WASM MEMFS.
